Weekly Review: Jørgen | June 21–27, 2026
1. Shift Impact
One day shift (June 21), then six vacation days with no shift constraint. June 21's sleep score was the week's worst at 47 (4h22, bed 01:32→wake 05:55), driven by the early 07:30 start. From June 22 onward, sleep scores climbed steadily — 51, 60, 80, 70, 76, and finally 86 on June 27. Bedtime drifted progressively later through the week (01:32 → 02:05 → 05:29 → 03:36 → 01:31 → 03:52 → 03:39) — entirely without shift pressure. This settles the open question from a couple weeks back: the drift isn't shift-driven, it's a vacation/light/value-driven pattern. A soft bedtime ceiling (~00:30) is the right next experiment rather than a hard sleep-by rule.
2. Training
Light through most of the week, then one big effort. June 21: 6.3km run, HR133 avg, 73% Zone 2 — clean execution. June 22, 24, 25, 26 were rest days. June 23 had a short 2km walk and an easy indoor bike session (HR119, mostly Z2). June 27: a 49.2km tempo ride, 2h07, HR157 avg, 59% Zone 4 — the week's dominant session, load 285. This rode a genuine PRIME-readiness window and was a well-grounded call given cycling's low Achilles risk. Acute load (397) now sits above chronic load (322), ACWR 1.2 — expected after a single hard session, not a trend.
3. Nutrition
Highly variable, vacation-typical. June 22 is the clear outlier: 3,661 kcal, 551.9g carbs, 269.8g sugar, only 103.1g protein — no training to justify it. June 25 and 26 sat well under target (1,658 and 1,470 kcal). June 27 (tempo day) logged 2,713 kcal with 401.7g carbs and 107.8g protein — consistent with the big-training-day rule of eating back roughly a quarter to a third of the burn, weighted to carbs. Best protein days: June 21 (217.4g, on a shift day) and June 26 (175.7g). No day hit both calorie and protein targets together — the week reads as reactive eating rather than planned intake.
4. Recovery
HRV trended upward across the week — 72 → 71 → 69 → 74 → 69 → 74 → 76 — peaking on June 27, right before the tempo effort. RHR stayed in a tight, healthy band (43–47, avg 44). Stress climbed slightly toward the back half (21 → 37) alongside the harder ride. Readiness swung widely and ended the week on its worst point — averaging 40.4 across the week but dropping from 32 (LOW) on June 21 to 13 (POOR) by June 27, a net -19 over the week. Recovery time jumped to 96h after the tempo ride. That end-of-week crash is the system working as intended after a hard effort, not a concerning trend.
5. Key Takeaway
The sleep-drift question is answered — it's a free-time/light pattern, not a shift problem. Bedtime drifted later through a week with zero shift pressure. Combined with the 96h recovery window opened by Saturday's tempo ride, the days ahead are a good stretch for something other than running — Day B (bodyweight strength) is still untouched and fits well here, being low-impact on both the Achilles and the cycling recovery.
6. Body Composition / Trend
Daily weigh-ins this week: 75.89kg (Jun 22) → 76.46kg (Jun 23) → 75.81kg (Jun 24) → 76.3kg (Jun 25) → 76.04kg (Jun 26) → 75.55kg (Jun 27). No weigh-in logged June 21. Noisy day-to-day (normal — hydration, food timing, the tempo ride's effect on water%), but the trend across the week is flat-to-slightly-down, ending at the week's lowest reading (75.55kg) alongside the week's lowest body fat (24.4%, down from 24.5–25.2% mid-week). Muscle mass held steady in a tight 32.23–32.45kg band throughout. Encouraging finish to a vacation week that included one genuinely loose nutrition day.
